trivia_bullets.text: Battle for Zendikar (October 2015) depicts the Eldrazi titan Ulamog's devastation of Zendikar after being freed from imprisonment. The plane's survivors — including the Gatewatch, formed in this block — mount a desperate resistance. Nablus, the flavor-text speaker, is named as a North Hada trapper, placing the card's perspective in Akoum, the continent most directly ravaged by Ulamog's emergence. [Scryfall: set_name Battle for Zendikar, released_at 2015-10-02; MTG Salvation: World of Zendikar Part V, via WebSearch snippet]
sources: MTG Salvation: World of Zendikar Part V, via WebSearch snippet

trivia_bullets.text: Flavor text: "Zendikar is always angry, though I've never seen that anger so precisely directed." — Nablus, North Hada trapper. [Scryfall]

Rolling Thunder is a reprint that originated in Tempest (October 1997) — making it nearly two decades old when reprinted in BFZ. Full print run: Tempest (1997), Battle Royale Box Set (1999), Planechase (2009), Tempest Remastered (2015), Battle for Zendikar (2015), Game Night (2018), The List / PLST (2019). [Scryfall: reprint: true; Scryfall prints search oracle ID 9c47888b]

Rolling Thunder is a staple finisher in Pauper Tron (Mono-Green or Flicker-Tron variants), where the deck generates large quantities of colorless mana through Urza's Mine/Tower/Power Plant. With arbitrary mana, Rolling Thunder functions as both a board wipe against small creatures and a direct kill for the opponent. The 'divided as you choose among any number of targets' clause is the key flexibility that separates it from simpler X-damage spells. EDHREC rank 12,960 reflects meaningful play at the format level. [CoolStuffInc: 'The Many Faces of Pauper Tron' by Paige Smith, via WebSearch snippet; CoolStuffInc: 'The New Pauper Tron' by Alex Ullman, via WebSearch snippet; Scryfall: edhrec_rank: 12960; Scryfall: legalities — pioneer, modern, pauper, legacy, vintage all legal]
sources: CoolStuffInc Pauper Tron articles, via WebSearch snippet

related_cards.note: Original 1997 printing — same oracle text, different art

related_cards.name: Capsize
related_cards.note: Pauper Tron co-finisher; Rolling Thunder + Capsize is the classic Tron endgame loop

related_cards.name: Urza's Mine
related_cards.note: Tron land; Rolling Thunder's power in Pauper is entirely dependent on Tron mana engine

related_cards.name: Fireball
related_cards.note: Earlier X-spell archetype; Rolling Thunder's 'any number of targets' is the design evolution beyond Fireball's 'one plus any number of creatures'
